entities.name: CONSTRUCTED WETLANDS
entities.description: Field-scale wastewater treatment systems used for removing pathogens from various wastewater sources.

entities.name: PATHOGEN REMOVAL
entities.description: The process of eliminating pathogenic microorganisms from wastewater to enable reuse.

entities.name: WASTEWATER TREATMENT
entities.description: The process of removing contaminants from wastewater.

entities.name: AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The sector where treated wastewater is potentially reused.

entities.name: FREE SURFACE FLOW
entities.description: A specific flow regime used in the constructed wetland systems.

entities.name: URBAN DOMESTIC WASTEWATER
entities.description: Wastewater sourced from urban residential areas.

entities.name: RURAL DOMESTIC WASTEWATER
entities.description: Wastewater sourced from rural residential areas.

entities.name: ESCHERICHIA COLI
entities.description: A bacterial indicator used to measure pathogen load in wastewater.

entities.name: ENTEROCOCCI
entities.description: A bacterial indicator used to measure pathogen load in wastewater.

entities.name: TOTAL COLIFORMS
entities.description: A group of bacteria used as indicators of water quality and pathogen presence.

entities.name: CONTINUOUS HYDRAULIC FLOW
entities.description: The operational state of water movement through the treatment systems.

entities.name: PCA
entities.description: Principal Component Analysis used to analyze the removal of bacterial indicators.

entities.name: THREE-COMPONENT ANALYSIS OF VARIANCE
entities.description: A statistical analysis of variance used within PCA to evaluate measured parameters.

entities.name: WATER QUALITY
entities.description: The chemical, physical, and biological characteristics of water.

entities.name: AGRO-CLIMATIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Variable environmental conditions affecting the performance of treatment systems.
